humanities_role_explanation: Designed for art-historical and cultural visual understanding, using curated museum collections from the Getty and Met as the primary data source to test the inference of non-observable cultural attributes.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal evaluation where models receive image-only inputs of cultural heritage objects and generate structured text predictions (JSON metadata) evaluated against textual ground truth.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluating the inference of non-observable historical and contextual attributes—such as cultural classification, historical period, geographic origin, and creator attribution—from visual evidence.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: It is a domain-specific benchmark focused specifically on cultural heritage metadata inference.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated nine vision-language models including Qwen-VL-Max, Qwen3-VL series, Pixtral-12B, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-5.4-mini, and Claude Haiku 4.5.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary systems being benchmarked are vision-language generative models (VLMs).

arts_humanities_data.note: Matches Visual Arts and Performing Arts, and Museology, utilizing curated museum collections (paintings, ceramics, metalwork, sculpture).

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluates performance using exact-match accuracy, partial-match accuracy, and attribute-level accuracy.

english_language.note: The paper and benchmark metadata are in English.

summary: The paper introduces Appear2Meaning, a cross-cultural benchmark designed to evaluate vision-language models on structured cultural metadata inference from image-only inputs. Using a dataset of 750 validated museum artifacts spanning four cultural regions and four object types, the benchmark measures the models' capacity to infer non-observable attributes like creator, origin, and period using an LLM-as-a-Judge framework.
key_findings: All evaluated VLMs perform poorly on exact match accuracy (ranging from 0.005 to 0.029), but show significantly higher partial match rates (up to 0.658 for Qwen3-VL-Flash). Models demonstrate substantial cultural variation, achieving stronger performance in East Asian contexts than European or American ones, and frequently default to Eurocentric stylistic shortcuts and period compression.
